content.method: Demographic microsimulation combining municipal-level birth-cohort projections (2025-2040) with statutory minimum class-size rules to forecast school-level viability thresholds; sensitivity analysis across fertility/migration scenarios.
content.question: At what local cohort size and under which demographic scenarios do peripheral Hungarian settlements become unable to sustain even one viable selective-track or comprehensive-school intake class, and how many settlements cross this threshold under current fertility trends?
content.why_it_matters: This directly determines the plausibility and geographic even-handedness of the passive-contraction baseline (T5) and shapes whether T2's capacity caps or T3's structural conversion need geographically targeted design rather than uniform national rules.
